title: LuhTech ComputedValue — Reusable Financial Type
description: Standalone, importable JSON Schema definition for the ComputedValue pattern. Every quantitative financial claim in the LuhTech portfolio is expressed as a ComputedValue: variables (sourced inputs) + formula (derivation) + result (cached output) + provenance metadata. An auditor can reproduce any number from schema data alone. Reference this schema via $ref from roadmap-business.schema.v3.0+.

ConfidenceLevel.description: Calibrated confidence in a value or computation. Must be set honestly — 'assumed' fields are never hidden.

x-enumDescriptions.verified: Externally confirmed with documentation. Third-party audit, signed contracts, bank statements, or government data. Reproduce-able by an auditor from source documents.
x-enumDescriptions.high: Strong external sourcing. Industry report, comparable deal data, or well-reasoned derivation from verified inputs. Unlikely to be materially wrong.
x-enumDescriptions.medium: Reasonable estimate with partial backing. Internal calculation based on high-confidence inputs, or benchmarked assumption with good comparables. Could be off by 20-40%.
x-enumDescriptions.low: Rough estimate. Limited external validation. Use with caution; flag to investors. Replace with better data at first opportunity.
x-enumDescriptions.assumed: Explicit assumption with no external validation. Founder judgment or analogy to comparables. MUST include formulaComment explaining basis and plan to replace.
x-enumDescriptions.internal: Internal decision or founder judgment, not derived from external data. Appropriate for planning values (e.g., target raise amount, option pool size, go-to-market timing).

SourceType.description: Classification of data source. 'assumed' and 'internal-calc' REQUIRE a comment field. 'internal-estimate' SHOULD include a comment.

x-enumDescriptions.verified: Audited or legally verified. Signed contracts, bank statements, government filings with legal standing.
x-enumDescriptions.reported: Self-reported by a company or organization. Press releases, annual reports, investor decks. Not independently audited.
x-enumDescriptions.industry-report: Third-party market research from a named research firm (MarketsandMarkets, Gartner, Forrester, IDC, etc.). Cite URL and report date.
x-enumDescriptions.government-data: Government statistical agencies (BLS, Census Bureau, BEA, Statistics Canada). High credibility for macro data.
x-enumDescriptions.comparable: Comparable company, deal, or transaction data. Must cite the comparable with public justification for why it applies.
x-enumDescriptions.news: News article, press coverage, or blog post. Lower credibility — use only for directional data or citations with named sources.
x-enumDescriptions.pilot-data: Data from active or completed pilot deployments within LuhTech portfolio. Highest quality for venture-specific operational claims.
x-enumDescriptions.internal-calc: Internal calculation derived from other sourced inputs. Formula must be in formulaComment. REQUIRES comment explaining derivation chain.
x-enumDescriptions.internal-estimate: Internal estimate from founder or team judgment. Not derived from external data. SHOULD include comment explaining basis.
x-enumDescriptions.assumed: Explicit assumption with no external or internal backing. Used as a placeholder pending better data. REQUIRES comment with replacement plan.
x-enumDescriptions.external-research: External research not from a formal named research firm. Academic papers, analyst blogs, conference presentations. Cite URL and author.
x-enumDescriptions.internal-ref: Value sourced from another field in the same or sibling roadmap-business.json. The 'name' field must contain the JSON path reference (e.g., 'financials.burnRate.result').

Source.description: Provenance record for a variable or computation. Appears as slide bylines, investor deck footnotes, and dataroom justification. Every number must be traceable to its source.

name.description: Full citation name. For industry reports: 'FirmName — Report Title Year'. For internal: 'Internal — brief description'. For internal-ref: 'financials.burnRate.result (see burnRate ComputedValue)'.
examples: Internal — market rate senior engineer benchmarked against Levels.fyi, San Diego CA, 2025
BLS Occupational Employment Statistics — Software Developers, May 2024

url.description: Source URL. Required for industry-report, government-data, external-research, and comparable sources. Omit for internal sources.

date.description: Date of the source data — 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'YYYY-Qn', or 'YYYY'. Use the date of the data, not the access date.

accessDate.description: Date the URL was accessed. Required when url is present. Enables staleness detection.

byline.description: Short attribution string for slides and reports. Keep under 60 characters. Examples: 'Source: MarketsandMarkets, 2024', 'Source: Internal Analysis, 2026'.

comment.description: Required when type is 'assumed', 'internal-calc', or 'internal-estimate'. Explains derivation basis, why this assumption was chosen, and the plan to replace it with better data. Also used for any additional context that aids reproducibility.

Variable.description: Named, typed, sourced input to a ComputedValue. Variables are the base data layer — the ONLY things that change when reality changes. A founder updates a variable; the formula re-derives the result. No other part of the system changes. Variable ids are used as operands in formula expressions.

id.description: Stable snake_case identifier. Used verbatim in formula expressions. Never change this without updating the formula.

label.description: Human-readable label for this variable. Appears in dataroom and audit context.

value.description: The numeric value of this variable. Always a number (integer or float). Non-numeric values belong in metadata fields, not variables.

unit.description: Unit of measurement using LuhTech unit vocabulary. Use canonical forms: 'USD', 'USD_per_month', 'USD_billions', 'USD_millions', 'percent', 'months', 'years', 'headcount', 'count', 'ratio'.

asOf.description: Date this value was last verified or collected. 'YYYY-MM-DD' or 'YYYY-Qn'. Used for staleness detection — values older than 90 days should be reviewed.

confidence.description: Confidence in this specific variable's accuracy. The ComputedValue's overall confidence should not exceed the lowest-confidence variable.

ScenarioVariant.description: A scenario variant of a ComputedValue. Contains the alternative variable values and the resulting recomputed result. The parent ComputedValue's variables array holds base-case values; ScenarioVariant overrides specific variable values for the scenario.

label.description: Human-readable scenario name, e.g. 'Optimistic: 3x faster pilot conversion'.

description.description: Narrative explaining what conditions make this scenario occur.

variables.description: ONLY the variables that differ from the base case. The formula is the same. The transform re-evaluates the formula with these override values merged into the base variables.

formula.description: Override formula for this scenario only. Omit if same as parent formula.

result.description: Pre-computed result for this scenario using the override variables.

resultUnit.description: Unit of the scenario result. Must match parent resultUnit unless formula also differs.

displayValue.description: Formatted display string for this scenario.

Scenarios.description: Optional scenario modeling container for a ComputedValue. Stores optimistic/base/pessimistic variants without duplicating the schema. The parent ComputedValue holds the base case in its variables[] and result fields. Scenarios contain only the override variables and recomputed results. DESIGN RULE: scenario variants use the same formula as the parent. If the formula changes, that is a different ComputedValue, not a scenario.

optimistic.description: Best-case scenario: favorable assumptions on key variables (e.g., higher conversion rate, faster hiring, lower churn).

base.description: Base case: same as parent ComputedValue variables[]. Included here optionally for explicit labeling in investor outputs.

pessimistic.description: Worst-case scenario: adverse assumptions on key variables (e.g., lower conversion, slower hiring, higher churn). Use for investor risk disclosure.

ComputedValue.description: A quantitative claim with full provenance. The core pattern of LuhTech financial architecture. RULE: no bare scalar financial values in roadmap-business.json — every number is a ComputedValue. Variables are inputs, formula is the derivation, result is the cached output. Change a variable — rerun the formula — result updates. The stored result is a performance cache. The formula + variables are the truth. An auditor must be able to reproduce the result from variables alone.

id.description: Stable snake_case identifier for this computed value. Used in graph references and transform field mappings. Never rename without a schema migration.

label.description: Human-readable label. Appears in investor dashboards and reports.

description.description: Extended description. Explains what this value represents, how it should be interpreted, and what it is NOT (disambiguation).

variables.description: All numeric inputs. These are the only levers. To update a projection, change a variable value and recompute. Minimum 1 variable required — even a single input value must be expressed as a variable to carry its source and confidence.

formula.description: Mathematical expression using variable ids as operands. Supported operators: + - * / ^ (power) and parentheses. Must be evaluatable by a JSONata-compatible expression engine. Examples: 'infra_monthly_usd + founder_monthly_usd', 'sam_usd_billions * capture_rate', '((market_size_2030 / market_size_2024) ^ (1 / forecast_years) - 1) * 100'.

formulaComment.description: Plain-English explanation of the formula logic and its assumptions. Required when formula is non-trivial. Show the numeric substitution: 'Monthly burn = $172 (infra) + $6,320 (founder) = $6,492'. Include any constraints, floor/ceiling values, or conditions that affect interpretation.

result.description: Pre-computed output. MUST be recomputed whenever any variable changes. This is a performance cache — if variables and result are inconsistent, variables win. The transform engine re-derives results from variables at runtime.

resultUnit.description: Unit of the result using LuhTech unit vocabulary. Must match the dimensional analysis of the formula.

displayValue.description: Formatted string for slides, reports, and investor dashboards. DECOUPLED from result — a result of 750000 may display as '$500K–$1M' (range) or '$750K' (midpoint) depending on context. The transform layer controls which display value to use in which output.

confidence.description: Overall confidence in this computed value. Should not exceed the minimum confidence of its input variables. Be honest: an investor reading 'assumed' knows to probe this number.

byline.description: Attribution string aggregated from variable sources. Appears as footnote on investor slides. Format: 'Source: [Source1]; [Source2], [Year]'. Keep under 100 characters.

lastComputed.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of last formula evaluation. Used for staleness detection. Values not recomputed in 90 days should be flagged for review.

scenarios.description: Optional scenario modeling. Optimistic/base/pessimistic variants. Each variant overrides specific variables and provides the recomputed result. The parent ComputedValue always holds the base case.

stalenessPolicy.description: Optional staleness policy for this specific ComputedValue. Overrides global policy.

reviewAfterDays.description: Flag this value for review if lastComputed is older than this many days.

autoExpireAfterDays.description: Treat result as invalid if lastComputed exceeds this threshold. Forces recomputation from variables.

changes: Initial standalone ComputedValue schema extracted from roadmap-business.schema.v2.2
Added ConfidenceLevel enum with 6 levels: verified, high, medium, low, assumed, internal
Added SourceType enum with 12 types covering all LuhTech source classifications
Added ScenarioVariant and Scenarios types for optimistic/base/pessimistic modeling
Added stalenessPolicy for per-value review thresholds
Added accessDate to Source for URL staleness detection
Formalized unit vocabulary in Variable and ComputedValue
Added internal-ref SourceType for cross-document field references
